content.title:
Follow the Budget.
Find the *Story*
content.sub: Understanding how public money is planned, spent, and monitored.
content.promise: In the next 10 minutes, you will learn how Kenya's budget roadmap works and exactly where you fit in. No jargon. No confusion. Just the facts, in your language.

content.question:
Forget the complicated name.
The BPS is simply the government answering *3 questions* out loud:

bullets.text: Where are we now? How's the economy doing?
Where do we want to go? What are our big goals?
How much cash will we need? And how will we get it?

content.badge: 💡 Key Fact: It's tabled in Parliament every year by February 15th—think of it as the government submitting its term paper before the final exam (which is the actual budget in April).

content.question: Why should *you* care about the BPS?

bullets.text: The BPS is the parent of every County Fiscal Strategy Paper (CFSP)
Whatever the BPS prioritizes at the national level... that's what your county government has to align with
This affects local markets, health centers, roads in your community
When you understand the BPS, you can track how money flows to your county

content.question:
The BPS is the *roadmap*
Before a long journey.
The budget in April is the actual *trip.*

bullets.text: Just like your maps app, it updates every year based on new economic data
The BPS also sets budget ceilings for ministries, parliament, the judiciary, and other govt departments
This prevents agencies from over-budgeting beyond what's available

content.headline:
The 5 Core Chapters
of a BPS

chapters.label: Vision
chapters.name: Development Agenda
chapters.desc: What the government wants to achieve

chapters.label: Reality
chapters.name: Economic Outlook
chapters.desc: How the economy is actually performing

chapters.label: Numbers
chapters.name: Government Spending & Revenue
chapters.desc: Billions and trillions: where money comes from and goes

chapters.label: Counties
chapters.name: Devolution Allocation
chapters.desc: How much each county government gets

chapters.label: Risks
chapters.name: Debt & Climate
chapters.desc: What could go wrong and why we need to prepare

stats.label: Debt Interest
stats.desc: Money already committed to paying back debt

content.headline: The 5 Pillars of BPS 2026
content.sub: What the government is betting on in 2026:

pillars.title: Agriculture
pillars.desc: Irrigation, crop diversification, improved value chains

pillars.title: MSMEs
pillars.desc: Hustler Fund expansion, business hubs in all 47 counties

pillars.title: Healthcare
pillars.desc: Universal Health Coverage for 35 million Kenyans

pillars.title: Digital & Creative
pillars.desc: Fibre internet + backing film, music, fashion

content.question:
What could go wrong?
What should we watch for?

bullets.text: Revenue gaps: If businesses struggle, taxes drop
Debt pressure: If the shilling weakens, debt gets expensive
Devolution drama: Counties' unpaid bills pile up; can't go to roads, teachers, or healthcare

content.badge: 💡 Why This Matters: When you know the risks, you can spot them early and ask the right questions before crisis hits. This is mature budgeting.

content.question:
Let's see if you can explain the BPS to your crew.

The BPS is best described as:

options.text: The final, detailed list of every government project
The government's strategic roadmap setting priorities and spending limits

feedback.correct: ✓ Exactly! You got it. The BPS is a strategic roadmap—the big picture before the detailed budget.
feedback.wrong: Try again. Think about what the BPS does—it sets the roadmap and priorities for the year.

content.title:
You finished!
*Stories Module*
content.sub: Great job! You now understand how Kenya's budget roadmap works. Ready to use your voice?

actions.title: Track the Budget
actions.sub: Check if projects in your county match BPS priorities
